raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I prepaid {$360.00} for a 4-day XXXX rental ( reservation # XXXX, rental agreement # XXXX ) in XXXX XXXX XXXX The car broke down mid-rental, forcing me to replace it at the XXXX XXXX return location and rent another XXXX car for {$87.00} just to get home. 

Instead of helping, XXXX added an additional {$930.00} charge to my account, plus duplicate {$87.00} charges ( only one refunded ) and a {$560.00} charge that was later reversed without explanation. When I log into my XXXX account, this rental does not appear in my history and I can not look it up by confirmation or rental agreement number. XXXX has stopped responding to my emails, and multiple promised manager call-backs and refunds never happened. 

I disputed the charges with American Express. While Amex refunded the prepaid {$360.00} and one {$87.00} charge, they continue to side with XXXX on the {$930.00} despite clear evidence of mechanical failure. Worse, Amex has repeatedly closed the dispute prematurely under the wrong reason, forcing me to reopen it multiple times. 

Amex also repeatedly asked me to follow up with XXXX directly, even though I had already made multiple unsuccessful calls where XXXX gave no resolution and then stopped responding entirely. I reached out to Amex because XXXX was unresponsive, yet Amex still insisted that I continue chasing XXXX myself rather than conducting a proper investigation. 
In addition, Amex failed to prevent further random charges by XXXX. XXXX continued billing my card until I specifically requested Amex block XXXX as a merchant. Only after my request did the repeated billing stop. 

Amexs handling of this dispute has been inadequate : they have dismissed evidence ( photos, call transcripts, missing rental record ), relied vaguely on terms and conditions without citing what terms apply, asked me to do their follow-up work with the merchant, and allowed XXXX to keep billing me without oversight. 

I am seeking resolution of the {$930.00} charge and a proper investigation into XXXX dispute handling and merchant monitoring failures.
